title: AI labs set Q2 lobbying records — Anthropic outspends Nvidia
subtitle: Federal disclosures show Anthropic at $1.97M (+26% QoQ), OpenAI at $1.2M (+18%), and Meta topping the sector at $5.99M as Washington's AI rulemaking accelerates.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Q2 2026 federal lobbying disclosures filed on July 20 show OpenAI and Anthropic each hitting record spending, with the two labs together outlaying $3.17 million from April through June. Anthropic reported $1.97 million — a 26% jump from Q1 and enough to outspend Nvidia and nearly match Oracle's $2 million. OpenAI reported $1.2 million, up 18% quarter-over-quarter. Anthropic's first-half 2026 total already sits above $3.5 million, more than the $3.1 million it spent for all of 2025. Their filings list cybersecurity, copyright, cloud computing, and defense procurement as active policy priorities.

The bigger picture is that AI labs have moved from lobbying periphery to lobbying core. Meta topped the sector at $5.99 million, Amazon followed at $4.36 million, and Alphabet, Microsoft, and Nvidia rounded out $21.25 million in total tech-industry federal spending for the quarter. What is new is not Big Tech spending — that number has been steady — but the composition: pure-play AI labs now sit inside the same league as Meta and Amazon, and they are spending against a different agenda. Anthropic and OpenAI are lobbying to shape rules that will constrain them; Meta and Amazon are lobbying to protect existing revenue lines.

The spending sits alongside Anthropic's $20 million additional donation to Public First Action, announced July 21, and the Trump White House's pending 30-day voluntary frontier-review framework. Read together, the three data points describe a lab-driven push for federal rulemaking that preempts a state patchwork — California's frontier-safety bills, the Colorado AI Act replacement, and Illinois SB315 have all shipped in the past two months, and the industry would rather write one federal rule than fight fifty state ones. This is the same playbook social media used from 2019 to 2022, except AI labs are spending it front-loaded and in one direction.

For learners: when a company lobbies for regulation of its own industry, it usually means the regulation is coming anyway and the company wants to shape it. Track this in two ways. First, look at ratios — Anthropic's lobbying-to-revenue ratio is now roughly 20x OpenAI's per unit of revenue, which tells you Anthropic sees policy as a bigger risk. Second, read the disclosure forms directly — they are public at opensecrets.org — and note which specific bills each lab reports lobbying on. The bills companies talk about most in their filings are the bills they most expect to pass.

sources.title: OpenAI, Anthropic boost lobbying as legacy tech and defense spending slips — CNBC
